page_content:
WGSS Department Chair: Neda Atanasoski, Ph.D. AASD Department Chair: Sangeetha Madhavan, Ph.D.

The joint minor in Black Women's Studies focuses on the lives and experiences of women of Africa and the African Diaspora. As a specialty in the ﬁelds of Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies and African American Studies, it will provide students with tools for understanding the social and cultural contexts in which race, gender, class, sexuality, ethnicity, nation and other dimensions of difference intersect to influence the lives and experiences of Black women.

See the African American and Africana Studies Department (https:// academiccatalog.umd.edu/undergraduate/colleges-schools/behavioral- social-sciences/african-american-africana-studies/) and Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies Department (https://academiccatalog.umd.edu/ undergraduate/colleges-schools/arts-humanities/women-gender- sexuality-studies/) for faculty rosters.

To make an appointment to explore or declare the minor, go to http:// arhu.umd.edu/undergraduate/academics/minors/.
